steps.title: Identify the clause structures
steps.content: Before the blank is an independent clause: “an extra leap second is added.” The word “whenever” begins a dependent clause: “time based on the planet's rotation lags a full nine-tenths of a second behind time kept by precise atomic clocks.”

steps.title: Apply the subordinating conjunction rule
steps.content: A subordinating conjunction connects an independent clause to a dependent clause directly, with no punctuation. Choice D applies this rule. The other choices add punctuation that either breaks the connection (A's comma), incorrectly uses semicolon syntax meant for independent clauses (B), or fragments the sentence (C). That matches choice D — D is correct.
